operational_knowledge_units.title: Hot bins definition and urgency context
operational_knowledge_units.summary: Hot bins are highest-priority bins/totes requiring fast processing, e.g., when a trailer departs in ~10 minutes, those must be completed first.
observed_evidence: 02:17:19: "Hot bins are the highest priority bins."
02:17:23: "the ones that I need to get [done] fast."
02:17:31: Trailer leaving in 10 minutes; "I need those done."
relationships: tote -> may be designated as 'hot' when outbound deadline is imminent
priority status -> driven by operational deadlines
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Hot bins: the highest-priority bins/totes processed urgently, especially before outbound trailer pulls (e.g., 10-minute window).

operational_knowledge_units.title: Priority signaling via 'indicator' messages (hop in/hop in close)
operational_knowledge_units.summary: Hot status is signaled via API/socket 'indicator' messages ('hop in'/'hop in close'). These messages are exchanged to mark what is hot for prioritization.
observed_evidence: 02:17:40: "they send us a message saying what's [hot]" / "what's hopped in"
02:17:50: "once they hit hop in close or hop in..."; "message type is called the indicator"
relationships: indicator messages ('hop in'/'hop in close') -> mark hot items
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Hot designation is communicated via API/socket as 'indicator' messages: hop in/hop in close. These exchanged signals indicate which totes/bins are hot for prioritization.
